metadata.title:
IN LINE IN SPITE
OF RAIN-SPEAkiNG POST
PONED UNTIL MORE FAVOR
ORABLE WEATHER..

pages.raw_text:
A grand parade of union men,
1000 strong and bands paraded
the streets this morning in honor
of Labor day, set aside as their
own. 1t rained all the time, but
that didn't matter.


The line of march was from the
central hall on First av. down to
Sprague and east to Division st.
where it turned and passed along
Riverside av. cheered by the thou
sands OF people and sympathizers
who lined the street.
/ 1t was | long string of umbrellas.
The men marched in twos and by
actual time the march took fifty
minutes to pass the corner of Riv-
erside av. and Howard st.


Forty-six unions, every one in
the city, was represented. The $50
prize which was offered the union
which turned out the largest per-
centage of members, will undoubt-
edly go to Federal Union No. 1924,
which had In line 157 men out Of
membership OF POS.


FOi OHtG Ill It's history, Inc
Washington Water Power Co. rec
ognized the day as the union man )
and held up all its cars for 80 bin
utes during the parade. Superin-
tendent Billingley this afternoon
said:


Il gave strict orders that the line
of march should not be cut OFF by
any cars. There was some com
plant by patrons, but the officials
felt that what little these people
were put out and the shatering OF
our time schedule which resulted,
was not to be compared with the
trouble which would result had we
run our cars"


The Traction company also held
up its cars.


The pressmen were accompanied
by regulation printers devils,
They ought to get the prize.
The teamsters were in line with
wagons and teams and appeared
with some fine looking stock. The
bartenders presented a cookbook
ing front with their blue and white
striped umbrellas.


Then followed the cooks and
bakers in white linen suits. With
the varied costumes and the black
umbrellas. the line presented pic
turesque front and was cheered to
the echo.


Only union turned out float
